commit.message:
feat: replace bot with Nuxt 3 web app (Telegram Mini App + WebView)

Replaces the old Netlify-based Telegram bot with a full Nuxt 3 web
application that serves as both the Telegram Mini App (opened by the
bot) and the target URL for the Flutter mobile WebView shell.

Stack: Nuxt 3 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn-vue + Pinia + Axios
       + @twa-dev/sdk + vue-leaflet

Features:
- Auth: login, register, email verification
- Events: list, detail, join/leave
- Map: alumni map with city markers (vue-leaflet)
- Profile: view and edit (avatar, bio, location, Telegram alias)

Deployment:
- Docker: Node.js server on port 3000 (same pattern as frontend)
- docker-stack.yml: service 'mobile' on iu_alumni_network (replaces
  Flutter web deployment at mobile.*.escalopa.com)
- GitHub Actions CI/CD: PR to main → testing, push to main → production

commit.message:
fix: split PGLITE_CHUNK_IDS=100 / PGLITE_CHUNK_CONTENT=25

The WASM stack overflow is triggered by result DATA SIZE, not just
parameter count. 200 params × ~946 chars/atom = ~189KB through WASM
stack — causes __emscripten_memcpy_js overflow and corrupts PGlite state
(all subsequent queries fail, engine crashes).

Two-tier chunking:
- PGLITE_CHUNK_CONTENT = 25  (content queries: ~25KB/batch, safe margin)
- PGLITE_CHUNK_IDS     = 100 (ID/tag-only queries: small result rows)

fetchNodes now uses separate loops: content at chunk=25, tags at chunk=100.
bfsViaEdges and bfsViaTags frontier chunked at 100 (ID-only results).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: robust session recovery when copilot server evicts idle sessions

When the copilot server evicts an idle session (~30 min timeout),
SendPrompt now has a 3-phase recovery:

1. SDK resume (fast path) — works if session state is still on disk
2. Retry the prompt — catches the case where resume 'succeeds' but
   the server-side session is actually gone
3. Rebuild with history — creates a brand-new session seeded with
   the full conversation history, like a transparent fork

The user's message goes through on the rebuilt session with no error
state. The conversation continues seamlessly.

Previously, phase 2 would fail with the same 'Session not found' and
the session would enter a permanent error state with no way to recover.

Root cause: the copilot server evicted session f61cef46 after ~40 min
idle, SDK ResumeSession returned the same dead session ID, and the
retry loop never fell through to a rebuild.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix search results not appearing (broken regex in template literal)

The highlight regex used \] and \/ inside a template literal which
dropped the backslashes in output, causing a JS SyntaxError that
silently killed the entire search script. Replaced with a simple
alphanumeric strip which avoids the escaping issue entirely.

commit.message:
Replace global @testing-library/react mock with explicit renderWithQueryClient helper (#2008)

The global `vi.mock("`@testing-library/react`")` in `vitest.setup.ts`
silently wraps every `render()` call with a new `QueryClientProvider`,
altering core testing API semantics suite-wide. This creates remount
artifacts on rerender (documented workaround in
`PipelineDetailPanel.test.tsx`) and hidden coupling in all tests.

### Changes

- **Removed global mock** from `vitest.setup.ts` — `render` now has
standard RTL semantics
- **Added `renderWithQueryClient` helper** in `tests/helpers/` using
RTL's native `wrapper` option, so `rerender()` reuses the same provider
instance
- **Migrated 18 component test files** from implicit global wrapping to
explicit helper usage
- **Simplified `DiscoverPage.test.tsx`** — replaced manual
`QueryClient`/`QueryClientProvider` setup with the shared helper
- **Updated `PipelineDetailPanel.test.tsx`** — removed stale comment
referencing the mock workaround

### Usage

```tsx
// Before: render() silently wrapped by global mock — no QueryClient visible at call site
render();

// After: explicit, uses RTL wrapper option (rerender-safe)
import { renderWithQueryClient } from "../helpers/renderWithQueryClient";
renderWithQueryClient();
```

Tests that don't need React Query use plain `render()` from
`@testing-library/react` unchanged.

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1996

---
The body of this PR [is automatically
managed](https://ela.st/github-ai-tools) by the [Update PR Body
workflow](https://github.com/elastic/ai-github-actions-playground/actions/runs/22811551049).

gpt-5.3-codex, id: 22811551049, workflow_id: gh-aw-update-pr-body, run:
https://github.com/elastic/ai-github-actions-playground/actions/runs/22811551049
-->

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: strawgate <6384545+strawgate@users.noreply.github.com>

Decompose usePageFiltersStore into domain-scoped stores with reset registry (#2009)

`usePageFiltersStore` merged five unrelated domain slices (Fleet,
Profiling, Services, Kubernetes, Hosts) into one Zustand singleton,
making it a parallel-change hotspot where PRs touching different domains
collide in the same file. The same coupling extended to
`storeResetters.ts`, where per-domain resets were hard-coded.

### Domain-scoped stores

Split into five independent stores, each owning its own state, actions,
and types:

- `useFleetFiltersStore` — tabs, auto-refresh, agent filters
- `useProfilingFiltersStore` — filters, raw query, view mode, stacktrace
IDs
- `useServiceFiltersStore` — inventory filters, search session
- `useKubernetesFiltersStore` — cluster/namespace/tab filters
- `useHostsFiltersStore` — OS filter, search, time range

### Registration-based reset composition

New `resetRegistry.ts` — stores self-register at import time:

```ts
// in useFleetFiltersStore.ts
registerResetter("fleet", () => useFleetFiltersStore.getState().resetFleetFilters());
```

`storeResetters.ts` now consumes `getRegisteredResetters()` instead of
hard-coding domain references. Adding a new domain store no longer
requires editing the central reset list.

### Consumer migration

All 11 source consumers and 9 test files were migrated to import from
their domain store directly. `usePageFiltersStore.ts` is retained as a
backward-compat barrel that re-exports only shared types
(`ProfilingViewMode`, `FleetViewTab`, `AgentFilter`).

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1991

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cfl: rewrite icc_dump_fuzzer V1→V2 — fix 4 timeout vectors

V1 vulnerabilities:
- O(N²) tag overlap detection (nested loop, 200 tags = 40K comparisons)
- Unbounded Describe() output (NamedColor2: 32K entries × 100B = 3.2MB)
- No tag count limit (200+ tags cause excessive iteration)
- No OOM guard (no icRealloc cap, no pre-scan validation)

V2 fixes:
- O(N logN) overlap via sorted offsets + std::upper_bound
- 256KB Describe() output cap (matches deep_dump_fuzzer)
- Tag count cap at 200 with pre-scan rejection
- icRealloc override (256MB cap) + allocation pre-scan
- Dual parse paths: ValidateIccProfile + OpenIccProfile (tool fidelity)
- build.sh: --allow-multiple-definition for icRealloc override

Counters: 97,143 → 97,543

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: discover agency marketplace plugins with agency.json convention (#508) (#509)

* feat: discover agency marketplace plugins with agency.json convention (#508)

Add support for discovering marketplace-installed plugins that use the
agency.json convention with plain .md agent files in agents/ subdirectories.

- Add AgencyPlugin interface for marketplace plugin metadata
- Add discoverAgencyPlugins() to scan installed-plugins/ for agency.json
- Add tryLoadAgencyPlugin() with copilot engine validation
- Enhance parseAgentFile() to handle YAML frontmatter (--- delimited)
- Update toKebabId() and readAndPushAgent() for .md extension stripping
- Integrate into discoverAgentsInCopilotDir() alongside existing *.agent.md scan
- Add 12 new test cases covering all edge cases

Closes #508

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace AgencyPlugin with generic ManifestResolver pattern

Replace hardcoded agency-specific plugin discovery with a resolver chain:

- AgencyPlugin interface -> generic PluginManifest interface
- New ManifestResolver interface (id, canResolve, resolve)
- Agency-specific logic extracted into agencyResolver (first resolver)
- discoverAgencyPlugins() -> discoverPlugins() iterating resolver chain
- tryLoadAgencyPlugin() removed; logic lives in agencyResolver.resolve()
- Exported ManifestResolver + registerResolver() for future marketplaces

Same directory walking, same flat + nested support, same deduplication.
All 1239 tests pass with zero changes to test files.

Implements architecture from rick-generic-plugin-resolver-architecture.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: update tests for ManifestResolver pattern (#508)

- Import and test PluginManifest, ManifestResolver, registerResolver, agencyResolver
- Rename 'agency marketplace plugins' describe to 'ManifestResolver plugin discovery'
- Add agencyResolver unit tests: interface compliance, canResolve, resolve edge cases
- Add resolver chain tests: first-match-wins, unknown format skipped, custom resolver
- Update agency-surface-audit.test.ts with allowlist for legitimate marketplace refs
- Export agencyResolver from agent-discovery.ts
- All 1253 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove blanket SKIP_FILES for agent-discovery.ts in audit

Replace the broad file-level skip with a targeted ALLOWED_PATTERNS entry
for the agencyJson local variable. The existing patterns already covered
agencyResolver, agency.json, and 'agency' literals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolver-first discovery, no double-scan, metadata flow

Address Rick's PR #509 review findings:

1. CRITICAL — Fix double-discovery bug: Run resolver chain BEFORE the
   recursive *.agent.md scan. Directories claimed by a resolver are added
   to a skipDirs set so collectAgentMdFilesRecursive skips them entirely.
   Resolvers are now the PRIMARY discovery mechanism for manifest plugins.

2. Extend DiscoveredAgent with optional 'marketplace' and 'resolverSource'
   fields. readAndPushAgent accepts an optional PluginManifest and flows
   the metadata through to downstream consumers.

3. Add 4 new tests exercising resolver-first semantics:
   - Resolver-claimed dir is NOT double-discovered (no duplicates)
   - Resolver metadata (marketplace, resolverSource) flows to DiscoveredAgent
   - Recursive scan still works for dirs NOT claimed by a resolver
   - Traditional agents have undefined resolver fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix cone-twist joint type mismatch: map desc type 1 to PHYS_JOINT_CONE_TWIST

The Blender export writes type=1 for 'Cone Twist' joints, but the
bone_joint_desc_t header documented type 1 as 'ball' and the adapter
mapped it to PHYS_JOINT_BALL. Ball joints only constrain position
(3 rows) with no angular limits, so all cone-twist joints had zero
angular constraint — bodies rotated freely and the mesh separated.

Fix: change adapter case 1 to create PHYS_JOINT_CONE_TWIST with
limit_min/max/axes propagated from the joint descriptor. Update
bone_joint_desc.h documentation to reflect type 1 = cone_twist.

Also includes re-exported humanoid skeleton with tighter joint limits,
ghost root body, and increased damping from the Blender side.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

at serve time via a <script> block. This eliminates the exposed token
endpoint and raw token in API responses flagged in PR #44 review.

Also removes --tenant from devtunnel access create so tunnel access
defaults to private (owner-only) instead of tenant-wide.

* fix: async-safe session reaper, deterministic DevTunnelLocator tests, ArchiveDir casing

  DevTunnelLocator for deterministic testing. Five new tests replace
  the single environment-dependent test.

  - set_print_option.option   → PrintOption
  - send_ams_control_command.cmd → AMSControlCommand
  - set_ams_user_setting.setting → AMSUserSetting
  - set_nozzle_details.nozzle_type → NozzleType (minus UNKNOWN)
  - set_nozzle_details.nozzle_diameter → NozzleDiameter values (minus 0.0)

Hardcoded (no BPM enum exists):
  - set_speed_level.level     → quiet/standard/sport/ludicrous
  - set_light_state.state     → on/off

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update DEPLOYMENT_DIAGNOSIS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix camera service: install system Python deps in bootstrap, guard cv2/numpy imports

- Bootstrap now installs python3-opencv, python3-flask, python3-numpy, python3-yaml via apt
  (pip install opencv-python-headless frequently fails on Pi ARM)
- Guard cv2/numpy imports with clear error messages instead of bare ImportError crash
- Root cause: camera service was crashing immediately because cv2 wasn't installed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace plain HTML site with React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui SPA

- Remove all old HTML pages (index, about, articles, projects, trading) and css/js dirs
- Add React 18 + TypeScript + Vite 6 + Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui setup
- Add React Router v7.12.0 SPA with Layout, Home, Articles, Projects, Trading, About pages
- Add markdown content files for articles, projects, and trading posts
- Add data loaders with marked + highlight.js + js-yaml frontmatter parsing
- Add GitHub Actions workflow for automated deploy to GitHub Pages
- Upgrade react-router to 7.12.0 to patch XSS vulnerabilities (CVE fixes)
